treatment_information.treatment_response: Treatment was successful, no trypanosomes were detected in the blood or CSF, fewer than 5 cells/mm3 were found in the CSF, and his general clinical condition was normal.

treatment_timeline.event: Patient admitted to the psychiatric department of the Regional Hospital of Banfora for psychiatric disorders.
HAT RDT (SD Bioline HAT) was performed and the patient turned out to be positive.
Positive TL test using the LiTat 1.3 variant antigenic type (VAT) performed on blood that had been dried on filter paper.
Positive Card Agglutination Test for Trypanosomiasis (CATT) preformed on whole blood and an end titer of 1/32 in CATT performed with twofold plasma dilutions (CATT pl).
Parasitological investigations revealed the presence of trypanosomes in the blood and in the cerebrospinal fluid (CSF).

patient_outcome.final_status: Treatment was successful, no trypanosomes were detected in the blood or CSF, fewer than 5 cells/mm3 were found in the CSF, and his general clinical condition was normal.
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: 6-month post-therapeutic follow-up confirmed that treatment was successful.
